findings.title: Exposed Environment File
findings.description: The file /.env is publicly accessible over HTTP on 192.168.10.50. The response body contained plaintext database connection strings, third-party API keys, and application secrets. Any unauthenticated attacker with network access to port 80 can retrieve this file.
findings.recommendation: Remove the .env file from the document root, or configure the web server to deny direct access to dotfiles. Rotate all credentials and secrets that were present in the file immediately.

evidence.description: GET /.env returned HTTP 200 OK. Response body (3,412 bytes) contained DB_CONNECTION=mysql, APP_KEY=base64:... and STRIPE_SECRET_KEY.

findings.title: Unauthenticated Redis Access
findings.description: Redis on 192.168.10.55:6379 accepts connections without authentication. An attacker can read all stored keys, write arbitrary data, flush the database, or achieve remote code execution via the CONFIG SET dir/dbfilename mechanism.
findings.recommendation: Bind Redis to 127.0.0.1 or a management VLAN interface only. Enable requirepass authentication. Disable the CONFIG command for untrusted clients. Place the service behind a firewall rule permitting only authorised application servers.

evidence.description: TCP connection to 6379 succeeded; PING returned +PONG; KEYS * returned 247 keys without authentication.

findings.title: RDP NLA Disabled
findings.description: Remote Desktop Protocol on 192.168.10.60 does not enforce Network Level Authentication. The RDP greeting is presented before any credential challenge, exposing the system to pre-authentication exploits.
findings.recommendation: Enable Network Level Authentication on all RDP-accessible hosts via Group Policy. Restrict RDP access to authorised management networks via firewall ACLs.

evidence.description: rdp-ntlm-info script returned Target_Name: EXAMPLECORP, Product_Version: 10.0.19041. NTLMSSP flags indicate NLA not enforced.

findings.title: SMBv1 Enabled
findings.description: SMBv1 is enabled on 192.168.10.60. This legacy protocol contains critical vulnerabilities including MS17-010 (EternalBlue), enabling unauthenticated remote code execution. SMBv1 is deprecated.
findings.recommendation: Disable SMBv1 on all Windows hosts via PowerShell: Set-SmbServerConfiguration -EnableSMB1Protocol $false. Block SMBv1 traffic at the network layer.

evidence.description: smb-security-mode script confirmed SMBv1 is negotiated. SMBv2 is also present but v1 is not disabled.

findings.title: Known Vulnerable Component Detected
findings.description: nginx 1.18.0 on 192.168.10.50 matches the version range affected by CVE-2023-44487 (HTTP/2 Rapid Reset). This allows unauthenticated remote attackers to cause denial of service.
findings.recommendation: Upgrade nginx to 1.25.3 or later. As a temporary mitigation, limit HTTP/2 connection concurrency.

cves.description: The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly, as exploited in the wild.

evidence.description: nginx 1.18.0 version-range matched against CVE-2023-44487 vulnerable range (< 1.25.3).

findings.title: Expired TLS Certificate
findings.description: The TLS certificate on 192.168.10.50:443 expired on 2026-01-15. Clients enforcing certificate validity will reject the connection.
findings.recommendation: Renew the certificate immediately. Configure automated renewal to prevent recurrence.

findings.title: TLS 1.0 Supported
findings.description: TLS 1.0 is offered by 192.168.10.50:443. This protocol version has known weaknesses including BEAST and POODLE and was deprecated by RFC 8996.
findings.recommendation: Disable TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1. Enable only TLS 1.2 and TLS 1.3.

evidence.description: ssl-enum-ciphers listed TLSv1.0 with cipher suite TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA.

findings.title: Weak SSH Key Exchange Algorithm
findings.description: The SSH server on 192.168.10.55 advertises weak key exchange algorithms including diffie-hellman-group1-sha1 (Logjam-affected) and diffie-hellman-group14-sha1.
findings.recommendation: Remove weak KEX algorithms from sshd_config. Set KexAlgorithms to curve25519-sha256,ecdh-sha2-nistp256 and reload sshd.

findings.title: DNS Recursion Enabled
findings.description: The DNS server at 192.168.10.1 appears to allow recursive queries from arbitrary sources. Open resolvers can be abused as amplification reflectors in UDP-based DDoS attacks.
findings.recommendation: Restrict recursive queries to authorised client IP ranges. Deploy Response Rate Limiting (RRL).

evidence.description: A recursive query for google.com returned a valid ANSWER section without REFUSED, suggesting open recursion.

findings.title: Directory Listing Enabled
findings.description: The web server at 192.168.10.50 serves directory listings for /backup/ and /uploads/. An attacker can enumerate all hosted files and may discover sensitive data.
findings.recommendation: Disable autoindex. Ensure all directories have an index file or return 403 Forbidden.

evidence.description: GET /backup/ returned HTTP 200 with an HTML directory listing containing 12 files including database_backup_2026-04.sql.gz.
